revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox train |name =賢所乗御車 |image =Kashikodokoro Jogyosha 01.jpg |numberconstruction =self |trainlength =19,991 mm |width =2,590 mm |height =3,778 mm |carlength =19,748 mm |weight=31.49 t |carbody = wood |gauge =1,067 mm |bogies =three axles |brakes =[[Vacuum brake]] |manufacturer =[[Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Japanese Ministry of Railways]] Oy factory }} '''Kashikodokoro Riding Car''' (賢所乗御車, かしこどころじょうぎょしゃ) is a [[Passenger railroad car|''Passenger'' railroad car]] manufactured by the [[Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Japanese Ministry of Railways]] for the transportation of the sacred mirror, ''[[Yata no Kagami]]'' in 1915.<ref name="rj180-148"/>
== Background of Production == The sacred mirror, ''[[Yata no Kagami]]'' is one of the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures in Japan]]. During the [[Enthronement of the Japanese emperor]], the emperor must perform several ceremonies in front of this mirror.
When the Emperor before Emperor Taisho ascended to the throne, they lived in Kyoto, so there was no need to transport the sacred mirror far. When the Emperor moved from Kyoto to Tokyo, there was still no railroad connecting Kyoto and Tokyo, so the sacred mirror was carried by hand along with other sacred objects. Later, when the previous emperor died on July 30, 1912, the accession ceremony of Emperor Taisho, who succeeded to the throne, was to take place at the Kyoto Imperial Palace on November 10, 1915. This was because the [[:en:Imperial Household Law|Imperial Household Law]] of that time stipulated that the accession ceremony be held in Kyoto. This necessitated the temporary transfer of the sacred mirror needed for the ceremony to Kyoto. For this reason, a railroad car was built to carry the sacred mirror, along with car for the emperor and empress, a car for the empress, and a dining car.
There were three types of [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Imperial Regalia of Japan]]: mirror, sword, and jewel. Sword and jewel were loaded on the car for the emperor, but mirror was considered separate objects in which the god resided, so a special car was needed. <ref name="rj180-148"/>。
== Car overview == [[File:賢所乗御車図面.png|thumb|Top view drawing. Left is the front side.]]
===Interior=== In Japanese railroads, the entrance to the station platform is located on the left side of the train. The attendant and mirror also board this car from the left side.
The car is divided into seven rooms. A mirror is placed in the center (4th) room, and attendants ride in the three rooms in front of and behind the mirror room. At the back of the mirror room (on the opposite side of the entrance), there is a 385 mm wide passageway separated by a wall, allowing the attendant to go between the third and fifth rooms without passing through the mirror room.<ref name="rj180-148"/>
Rooms 1 and 2 have fixed long benches on both sides. Room 3 also has long benches, but with folding armrests, each can be separated into three seats. The surface materials of the attendant's room are [[oak]] and [[sawtooth oak]]. The ceiling panels are [[:en:Maple|Maple]], and the window frames are [[teak]].<ref name="rj180-149"/>
The fourth room is the room for the sacred mirror. The mirror room has 2,438 mm wide folding doors. The mirrors are put in and out through these doors. After the doors are closed, parts of the chrysanthemum flower, the imperial emblem, are attached to the joints. The mirror room has a [[:en:Coffered ceiling|Coffered ceiling]]. The interior is ''[[Shinden-zukuri]]'', and most of the material is just ''[[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki]]'' without paint. The metal parts are [[:en:Gold plated|Gold plated]].<ref name="rj180-148"/>
Room 5 has a total of four seats, two on each side.
Room 6 has a small room with a toilet and handwash stand in one corner. This toilet bowl is painted in black [[:en:Lacquer|Lacquer]]. The outside of the handwash bowl is painted with black lacquer and the inside with vermilion lacquer. It is equipped with a white wooden ladle.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
Room 7 has almost the same structure as room 1 and 2.
=== Estimate the mirror weight=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha 02.jpg|thumb|250px| Room for the sacred mirror]] The biggest concern for the Railway Institute's designers in building this car was the dimensions and weight of the sacred mirror.
The sacred mirror is an incarnation of the sun goddess ''[[Amaterasu]]'', and even the Crown Prince was not allowed to stand before it. Even if it was necessary by car design, the public was not allowed to see or touch it. When they asked for permission to measure, the [[Ministry of the Imperial Household]] initially refused. Later, only dimensional measurements were permitted, but weight measurements, which required lifting (touching) the sacred mirror and placing it on a scale, were finally not permitted.
The engineers therefore decided to extrapolate from previous literature. In 1868, this mirror was carried by people by hand from Kyoto to Tokyo. According to the records, : Sixteen young men carried the [[:en:en:Litter (vehicle)|en:Litter (vehicle)]] with the sacred mirror eastward on the ''[[Tōkaidō (road)|Tōkaidō]]'' road, all of them sweating from the weight of the litter. The weight of the sacred mirror was estimated from this description. They then designed the installation stand and transport equipment based on a fairly high estimate.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
===Bringing in the sacred mirror=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha and Ohaguruma 04.png|thumb|Transfer from litter to car (sectional drawing).]]
At the time of Emperor Showa's accession to the throne, a manual for transferring the mirror was prepared and careful practice was conducted. * Tokyo Station: 4 times in May, September, October, and November 1928. * Nagoya Station: October 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 18, 22, 26, November 5, 24. (13th and 18th are for trial run) * Oy Manufacturing Plant: 2 times each by Tokyo, Nagoya, and Osaka station staff upon completion of train refurbishment in October. * Trial runs between Tokyo and Kyoto: October 13–19 and 22–28.<ref name="sr130"/>
===Other=== The braking device is a [[vacuum brake]]<ref name="rj180-149"/>
== Operation == After its manufacture, it was used for the coronation ceremony of Emperor Taisho in 1915, carrying the sacred mirror from Tokyo to Kyoto and back; it was also used for the coronation ceremony of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]] in 1928, with an updated interior and other improvements.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
The decision was made to discontinue use of this car in October 1959. Therefore, this car carried the sacred mirror only four times, including the round trip on the occasion of the coronation ceremonies of the Taisho and Showa Emperors.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
After it was taken out of service, it was stored in the [[Takao Station (Tokyo)|Asakawa Garage]].<ref name="rj180-144"/>
== Afterward ==
In June 1963, since the Asakawa garage was to be closed, the car was moved to the Imperial garage in the Oy factory.<ref name="rj180-144"/>
In 1981, it was reported that the car smelled of cypress as one entered the interior.<ref name="rj180-148"/> Also, while cars for human built around the same time were converted to automatic air brakes, this car has been kept with vacuum brakes.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
In 2019, the law had changed, the [[Enthronement of the Japanese emperor]] took place in not Kyoto but Tokyo . The equipment for installing the sacred mirror during the accession ceremony, which had been permanently located in Kyoto, was transported to Tokyo. This eliminated the need to transport the sacred mirror by rail.
In 2023, the stored cars were moved before the demolition of the Imperial Garage began, but the new storage location has not been disclosed for security reasons.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/241525 |title=大正時代のれんが造り「御料車庫」の解体が始まる 失われる東京・品川の鉄道遺構 JR東日本が再開発 |trans-title= Dismantling of the Taisho Era brick Goryo Garage, a lost railroad structure in Shinagawa, Tokyo, to be redeveloped by East Japan Railway |publisher=東京新聞 |date=2023-04-02 |accessdate=2023-05-11}}</ref>
==References== {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="rj180-144">[[#Tanabe180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.144]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-148">[[#Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻 180号 p.148]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-149">[[# Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.149]]</ref> <ref name="sr130">[[# sRecord1|Ministry of Railways (1932) p.130]]</ref> }} * {{Cite journal|author=田邉幸夫 |year=1982 |volume=2|title=車両とともに30th year |trans-title= 30 years with the car|journal=鉄道ジャーナル |issue=180 |pages= 144–149 |publisher=鉄道ジャーナル社 |ref = Tanabe 180}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 附図 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor=[[Ministry of Railways (Japan)]] |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor Appendix|year=1930|page=20}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 上巻 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor volume 1|year=1932|ref=sRecord1}}
==See also== * [[Royal train]]

Even if it was necessary by car design, the public was not allowed to see or touch it. When they asked for permission to measure, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] initially refused. Later, only dimensional measurements were permitted, but weight measurements, which required lifting (touching) the sacred mirror and placing it on a scale, were finally not permitted.
The engineers therefore decided to extrapolate from previous literature. In 1868, this mirror was carried by people by hand from Kyoto to Tokyo. According to the records, : Sixteen young men carried the [[:en:en:Litter (vehicle)|en:Litter (vehicle)]] with the sacred mirror eastward on the ''[[Tōkaidō (road)|Tōkaidō]]'' road, all of them sweating from the weight of the litter. The weight of the sacred mirror was estimated from this description. They then designed the installation stand and transport equipment based on a fairly high estimate.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
===Bringing in the sacred mirror=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha and Ohaguruma 04.png|thumb|Transfer from litter to car (sectional drawing).]]
At the time of Emperor Showa's accession to the throne, a manual for transferring the mirror was prepared and careful practice was conducted. * Tokyo Station: 4 times in May, September, October, and November 1928. * Nagoya Station: October 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 18, 22, 26, November 5, 24. (13th and 18th are for trial run) * Oy Manufacturing Plant: 2 times each by Tokyo, Nagoya, and Osaka station staff upon completion of train refurbishment in October. * Trial runs between Tokyo and Kyoto: October 13–19 and 22–28.<ref name="sr130"/>
===Other=== The braking device is a [[vacuum brake]]<ref name="rj180-149"/>
== Operation == After its manufacture, it was used for the coronation ceremony of Emperor Taisho in 1915, carrying the sacred mirror from Tokyo to Kyoto and back; it was also used for the coronation ceremony of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]] in 1928, with an updated interior and other improvements.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
The decision was made to discontinue use of this car in October 1959. Therefore, this car carried the sacred mirror only four times, including the round trip on the occasion of the coronation ceremonies of the Taisho and Showa Emperors.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
After it was taken out of service, it was stored in the [[Takao Station (Tokyo)|Asakawa Garage]].<ref name="rj180-144"/>
== Afterward ==
In June 1963, since the Asakawa garage was to be closed, the car was moved to the Imperial garage in the Oy factory.<ref name="rj180-144"/>
In 1981, it was reported that the car smelled of cypress as one entered the interior.<ref name="rj180-148"/> Also, while cars for human built around the same time were converted to automatic air brakes, this car has been kept with vacuum brakes.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
In 2019, the law had changed, the [[Enthronement of the Japanese emperor]] took place in not Kyoto but Tokyo . The equipment for installing the sacred mirror during the accession ceremony, which had been permanently located in Kyoto, was transported to Tokyo. This eliminated the need to transport the sacred mirror by rail.
In 2023, the stored cars were moved before the demolition of the Imperial Garage began, but the new storage location has not been disclosed for security reasons.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/241525 |title=大正時代のれんが造り「御料車庫」の解体が始まる 失われる東京・品川の鉄道遺構 JR東日本が再開発 |trans-title= Dismantling of the Taisho Era brick Goryo Garage, a lost railroad structure in Shinagawa, Tokyo, to be redeveloped by East Japan Railway |publisher=東京新聞 |date=2023-04-02 |accessdate=2023-05-11}}</ref>
==References== {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="rj180-144">[[#Tanabe180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.144]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-148">[[#Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻 180号 p.148]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-149">[[# Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.149]]</ref> <ref name="sr130">[[# sRecord1|Ministry of Railways (1932) p.130]]</ref> }} * {{Cite journal|author=田邉幸夫 |year=1982 |volume=2|title=車両とともに30th year |trans-title= 30 years with the car|journal=鉄道ジャーナル |issue=180 |pages= 144–149 |publisher=鉄道ジャーナル社 |ref = Tanabe 180}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 附図 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor=[[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Ministry of Railways (Japan)]] |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor Appendix|year=1930|page=20}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 上巻 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor volume 1|year=1932|ref=sRecord1}}
==See also== * [[Royal train]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox train |name =賢所乗御車 |image =Kashikodokoro Jogyosha 01.jpg |numberconstruction =self |trainlength =19,991 mm |width =2,590 mm |height =3,778 mm |carlength =19,748 mm |weight=31.49 t |carbody = wood |gauge =1,067 mm |bogies =three axles |brakes =[[Vacuum brake]] |manufacturer =[[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Japanese Ministry of Railways]] Oy factory }} '''Kashikodokoro Riding Car''' (賢所乗御車, かしこどころじょうぎょしゃ) is a [[Passenger railroad car|''Passenger'' railroad car]] manufactured by the [[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Japanese Ministry of Railways]] for the transportation of the sacred mirror, ''[[Yata no Kagami]]'' in 1915.<ref name="rj180-148"/>
== Background of Production == The sacred mirror, ''[[Yata no Kagami]]'' is one of the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures in Japan]]. During the [[Enthronement of the Japanese emperor]], the emperor must perform several ceremonies in front of this mirror.
When the Emperor before Emperor Taisho ascended to the throne, they lived in Kyoto, so there was no need to transport the sacred mirror far. When the Emperor moved from Kyoto to Tokyo, there was still no railroad connecting Kyoto and Tokyo, so the sacred mirror was carried by hand along with other sacred objects. Later, when the previous emperor died on July 30, 1912, the accession ceremony of Emperor Taisho, who succeeded to the throne, was to take place at the Kyoto Imperial Palace on November 10, 1915. This was because the [[:en:Imperial Household Law|Imperial Household Law]] of that time stipulated that the accession ceremony be held in Kyoto. This necessitated the temporary transfer of the sacred mirror needed for the ceremony to Kyoto. For this reason, a railroad car was built to carry the sacred mirror, along with car for the emperor and empress, a car for the empress, and a dining car.
There were three types of [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Imperial Regalia of Japan]]: mirror, sword, and jewel. Sword and jewel were loaded on the car for the emperor, but mirror was considered separate objects in which the god resided, so a special car was needed. <ref name="rj180-148"/>。
== Car overview == [[File:賢所乗御車図面.png|thumb|Top view drawing. Left is the front side.]]
===Interior=== In Japanese railroads, the entrance to the station platform is located on the left side of the train. The attendant and mirror also board this car from the left side.
The car is divided into seven rooms. A mirror is placed in the center (4th) room, and attendants ride in the three rooms in front of and behind the mirror room. At the back of the mirror room (on the opposite side of the entrance), there is a 385 mm wide passageway separated by a wall, allowing the attendant to go between the third and fifth rooms without passing through the mirror room.<ref name="rj180-148"/>
Rooms 1 and 2 have fixed long benches on both sides. Room 3 also has long benches, but with folding armrests, each can be separated into three seats. The surface materials of the attendant's room are [[:en:Oak|Oak]] and [[sawtooth oak]]. The ceiling panels are [[:en:Maple|Maple]], and the window frames are [[teak]].<ref name="rj180-149"/>
The fourth room is the room for the sacred mirror. The mirror room has 2,438 mm wide folding doors. The mirrors are put in and out through these doors. After the doors are closed, parts of the chrysanthemum flower, the imperial emblem, are attached to the joints. The mirror room has a [[:en:Coffered ceiling|Coffered ceiling]]. The interior is ''[[Shinden-zukuri]]'', and most of the material is just ''[[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki]]'' without paint. The metal parts are [[:en:Gold plated|Gold plated]].<ref name="rj180-148"/>
Room 5 has a total of four seats, two on each side.
Room 6 has a small room with a toilet and handwash stand in one corner. This toilet bowl is painted in black [[:en:Lacquer|Lacquer]]. The outside of the handwash bowl is painted with black lacquer and the inside with vermilion lacquer. It is equipped with a white wooden ladle.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
Room 7 has almost the same structure as room 1 and 2.
=== Estimate the mirror weight=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha 02.jpg|thumb|250px| Room for the sacred mirror]] The biggest concern for the Railway Institute's designers in building this car was the dimensions and weight of the sacred mirror.
The sacred mirror is an incarnation of the sun goddess ''[[Amaterasu]]'', and even the Crown Prince was not allowed to stand before it. Even if it was necessary by car design, the public was not allowed to see or touch it. When they asked for permission to measure, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] initially refused. Later, only dimensional measurements were permitted, but weight measurements, which required lifting (touching) the sacred mirror and placing it on a scale, were finally not permitted.
The engineers therefore decided to extrapolate from previous literature. In 1868, this mirror was carried by people by hand from Kyoto to Tokyo. According to the records, : Sixteen young men carried the [[:en:en:Litter (vehicle)|en:Litter (vehicle)]] with the sacred mirror eastward on the ''[[Tōkaidō (road)|Tōkaidō]]'' road, all of them sweating from the weight of the litter. The weight of the sacred mirror was estimated from this description. They then designed the installation stand and transport equipment based on a fairly high estimate.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
===Bringing in the sacred mirror=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha and Ohaguruma 04.png|thumb|Transfer from litter to car (sectional drawing).]]
At the time of Emperor Showa's accession to the throne, a manual for transferring the mirror was prepared and careful practice was conducted. * Tokyo Station: 4 times in May, September, October, and November 1928. * Nagoya Station: October 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 18, 22, 26, November 5, 24. (13th and 18th are for trial run) * Oy Manufacturing Plant: 2 times each by Tokyo, Nagoya, and Osaka station staff upon completion of train refurbishment in October. * Trial runs between Tokyo and Kyoto: October 13–19 and 22–28.<ref name="sr130"/>
===Other=== The braking device is a [[vacuum brake]]<ref name="rj180-149"/>
== Operation == After its manufacture, it was used for the coronation ceremony of Emperor Taisho in 1915, carrying the sacred mirror from Tokyo to Kyoto and back; it was also used for the coronation ceremony of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]] in 1928, with an updated interior and other improvements.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
The decision was made to discontinue use of this car in October 1959. Therefore, this car carried the sacred mirror only four times, including the round trip on the occasion of the coronation ceremonies of the Taisho and Showa Emperors.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
After it was taken out of service, it was stored in the [[Takao Station (Tokyo)|Asakawa Garage]].<ref name="rj180-144"/>
== Afterward ==
In June 1963, since the Asakawa garage was to be closed, the car was moved to the Imperial garage in the Oy factory.<ref name="rj180-144"/>
In 1981, it was reported that the car smelled of cypress as one entered the interior.<ref name="rj180-148"/> Also, while cars for human built around the same time were converted to automatic air brakes, this car has been kept with vacuum brakes.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
In 2019, the law had changed, the [[Enthronement of the Japanese emperor]] took place in not Kyoto but Tokyo . The equipment for installing the sacred mirror during the accession ceremony, which had been permanently located in Kyoto, was transported to Tokyo. This eliminated the need to transport the sacred mirror by rail.
In 2023, the stored cars were moved before the demolition of the Imperial Garage began, but the new storage location has not been disclosed for security reasons.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/241525 |title=大正時代のれんが造り「御料車庫」の解体が始まる 失われる東京・品川の鉄道遺構 JR東日本が再開発 |trans-title= Dismantling of the Taisho Era brick Goryo Garage, a lost railroad structure in Shinagawa, Tokyo, to be redeveloped by East Japan Railway |publisher=東京新聞 |date=2023-04-02 |accessdate=2023-05-11}}</ref>
==References== {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="rj180-144">[[#Tanabe180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.144]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-148">[[#Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻 180号 p.148]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-149">[[# Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.149]]</ref> <ref name="sr130">[[# sRecord1|Ministry of Railways (1932) p.130]]</ref> }} * {{Cite journal|author=田邉幸夫 |year=1982 |volume=2|title=車両とともに30th year |trans-title= 30 years with the car|journal=鉄道ジャーナル |issue=180 |pages= 144–149 |publisher=鉄道ジャーナル社 |ref = Tanabe 180}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 附図 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor=[[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Ministry of Railways (Japan)]] |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor Appendix|year=1930|page=20}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 上巻 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor volume 1|year=1932|ref=sRecord1}}
==See also== * [[Royal train]]

{{Infobox train |name =賢所乗御車 |image =Kashikodokoro Jogyosha 01.jpg |numberconstruction =self |trainlength =19,991 mm |width =2,590 mm |height =3,778 mm |carlength =19,748 mm |weight=31.49 t |carbody = wood |gauge =1,067 mm |bogies =three axles |brakes =[[Vacuum brake]] |manufacturer =[[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Japanese Ministry of Railways]] Oy factory }} '''Kashikodokoro Riding Car''' (賢所乗御車, かしこどころじょうぎょしゃ) is a [[:en:Passenger railroad car|''Passenger'' railroad car]] manufactured by the [[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Japanese Ministry of Railways]] for the transportation of the sacred mirror, ''[[Yata no Kagami]]'' in 1915.<ref name="rj180-148"/>

Room 5 has a total of four seats, two on each side.
Room 6 has a small room with a toilet and handwash stand in one corner. This toilet bowl is painted in black [[:en:Lacquer|Lacquer]]. The outside of the handwash bowl is painted with black lacquer and the inside with vermilion lacquer. It is equipped with a white wooden ladle.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
Room 7 has almost the same structure as room 1 and 2.
=== Estimate the mirror weight=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha 02.jpg|thumb|250px| Room for the sacred mirror]] The biggest concern for the Railway Institute's designers in building this car was the dimensions and weight of the sacred mirror.
The sacred mirror is an incarnation of the sun goddess ''[[Amaterasu]]'', and even the Crown Prince was not allowed to stand before it. Even if it was necessary by car design, the public was not allowed to see or touch it. When they asked for permission to measure, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] initially refused. Later, only dimensional measurements were permitted, but weight measurements, which required lifting (touching) the sacred mirror and placing it on a scale, were finally not permitted.
The engineers therefore decided to extrapolate from previous literature. In 1868, this mirror was carried by people by hand from Kyoto to Tokyo. According to the records, : Sixteen young men carried the [[:en:en:Litter (vehicle)|en:Litter (vehicle)]] with the sacred mirror eastward on the ''[[Tōkaidō (road)|Tōkaidō]]'' road, all of them sweating from the weight of the litter. The weight of the sacred mirror was estimated from this description. They then designed the installation stand and transport equipment based on a fairly high estimate.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
===Bringing in the sacred mirror=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha and Ohaguruma 04.png|thumb|Transfer from litter to car (sectional drawing).]]
At the time of Emperor Showa's accession to the throne, a manual for transferring the mirror was prepared and careful practice was conducted. * Tokyo Station: 4 times in May, September, October, and November 1928. * Nagoya Station: October 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 18, 22, 26, November 5, 24. (13th and 18th are for trial run) * Oy Manufacturing Plant: 2 times each by Tokyo, Nagoya, and Osaka station staff upon completion of train refurbishment in October. * Trial runs between Tokyo and Kyoto: October 13–19 and 22–28.<ref name="sr130"/>
===Other=== The braking device is a [[vacuum brake]]<ref name="rj180-149"/>
== Operation == After its manufacture, it was used for the coronation ceremony of Emperor Taisho in 1915, carrying the sacred mirror from Tokyo to Kyoto and back; it was also used for the coronation ceremony of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]] in 1928, with an updated interior and other improvements.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
The decision was made to discontinue use of this car in October 1959. Therefore, this car carried the sacred mirror only four times, including the round trip on the occasion of the coronation ceremonies of the Taisho and Showa Emperors.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
After it was taken out of service, it was stored in the [[:en:Takao Station (Tokyo)|Asakawa Garage]].<ref name="rj180-144"/>
== Afterward ==
In June 1963, since the Asakawa garage was to be closed, the car was moved to the Imperial garage in the Oy factory.<ref name="rj180-144"/>
In 1981, it was reported that the car smelled of cypress as one entered the interior.<ref name="rj180-148"/> Also, while cars for human built around the same time were converted to automatic air brakes, this car has been kept with vacuum brakes.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
In 2019, the law had changed, the [[Enthronement of the Japanese emperor]] took place in not Kyoto but Tokyo . The equipment for installing the sacred mirror during the accession ceremony, which had been permanently located in Kyoto, was transported to Tokyo. This eliminated the need to transport the sacred mirror by rail.
In 2023, the stored cars were moved before the demolition of the Imperial Garage began, but the new storage location has not been disclosed for security reasons.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/241525 |title=大正時代のれんが造り「御料車庫」の解体が始まる 失われる東京・品川の鉄道遺構 JR東日本が再開発 |trans-title= Dismantling of the Taisho Era brick Goryo Garage, a lost railroad structure in Shinagawa, Tokyo, to be redeveloped by East Japan Railway |publisher=東京新聞 |date=2023-04-02 |accessdate=2023-05-11}}</ref>
==References== {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="rj180-144">[[#Tanabe180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.144]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-148">[[#Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻 180号 p.148]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-149">[[# Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.149]]</ref> <ref name="sr130">[[# sRecord1|Ministry of Railways (1932) p.130]]</ref> }} * {{Cite journal|author=田邉幸夫 |year=1982 |volume=2|title=車両とともに30th year |trans-title= 30 years with the car|journal=鉄道ジャーナル |issue=180 |pages= 144–149 |publisher=鉄道ジャーナル社 |ref = Tanabe 180}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 附図 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor=[[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Ministry of Railways (Japan)]] |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor Appendix|year=1930|page=20}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 上巻 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor volume 1|year=1932|ref=sRecord1}}
==See also== * [[Royal train]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox train |name =賢所乗御車 |image =Kashikodokoro Jogyosha 01.jpg |numberconstruction =self |trainlength =19,991 mm |width =2,590 mm |height =3,778 mm |carlength =19,748 mm |weight=31.49 t |carbody = wood |gauge =1,067 mm |bogies =three axles |brakes =[[:en:Vacuum brake|Vacuum brake]] |manufacturer =[[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Japanese Ministry of Railways]] Oy factory }} '''Kashikodokoro Riding Car''' (賢所乗御車, かしこどころじょうぎょしゃ) is a [[:en:Passenger railroad car|''Passenger'' railroad car]] manufactured by the [[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Japanese Ministry of Railways]] for the transportation of the sacred mirror, ''[[Yata no Kagami]]'' in 1915.<ref name="rj180-148"/>
== Background of Production == The sacred mirror, ''[[Yata no Kagami]]'' is one of the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures in Japan]]. During the [[Enthronement of the Japanese emperor]], the emperor must perform several ceremonies in front of this mirror.
When the Emperor before Emperor Taisho ascended to the throne, they lived in Kyoto, so there was no need to transport the sacred mirror far. When the Emperor moved from Kyoto to Tokyo, there was still no railroad connecting Kyoto and Tokyo, so the sacred mirror was carried by hand along with other sacred objects. Later, when the previous emperor died on July 30, 1912, the accession ceremony of Emperor Taisho, who succeeded to the throne, was to take place at the Kyoto Imperial Palace on November 10, 1915. This was because the [[:en:Imperial Household Law|Imperial Household Law]] of that time stipulated that the accession ceremony be held in Kyoto. This necessitated the temporary transfer of the sacred mirror needed for the ceremony to Kyoto. For this reason, a railroad car was built to carry the sacred mirror, along with car for the emperor and empress, a car for the empress, and a dining car.
There were three types of [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Imperial Regalia of Japan]]: mirror, sword, and jewel. Sword and jewel were loaded on the car for the emperor, but mirror was considered separate objects in which the god resided, so a special car was needed. <ref name="rj180-148"/>。
== Car overview == [[File:賢所乗御車図面.png|thumb|Top view drawing. Left is the front side.]]
===Interior=== In Japanese railroads, the entrance to the station platform is located on the left side of the train. The attendant and mirror also board this car from the left side.
The car is divided into seven rooms. A mirror is placed in the center (4th) room, and attendants ride in the three rooms in front of and behind the mirror room. At the back of the mirror room (on the opposite side of the entrance), there is a 385 mm wide passageway separated by a wall, allowing the attendant to go between the third and fifth rooms without passing through the mirror room.<ref name="rj180-148"/>
Rooms 1 and 2 have fixed long benches on both sides. Room 3 also has long benches, but with folding armrests, each can be separated into three seats. The surface materials of the attendant's room are [[:en:Oak|Oak]] and [[sawtooth oak]]. The ceiling panels are [[:en:Maple|Maple]], and the window frames are [[teak]].<ref name="rj180-149"/>
The fourth room is the room for the sacred mirror. The mirror room has 2,438 mm wide folding doors. The mirrors are put in and out through these doors. After the doors are closed, parts of the chrysanthemum flower, the imperial emblem, are attached to the joints. The mirror room has a [[:en:Coffered ceiling|Coffered ceiling]]. The interior is ''[[Shinden-zukuri]]'', and most of the material is just ''[[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki]]'' without paint. The metal parts are [[:en:Gold plated|Gold plated]].<ref name="rj180-148"/>
Room 5 has a total of four seats, two on each side.
Room 6 has a small room with a toilet and handwash stand in one corner. This toilet bowl is painted in black [[:en:Lacquer|Lacquer]]. The outside of the handwash bowl is painted with black lacquer and the inside with vermilion lacquer. It is equipped with a white wooden ladle.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
Room 7 has almost the same structure as room 1 and 2.
=== Estimate the mirror weight=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha 02.jpg|thumb|250px| Room for the sacred mirror]] The biggest concern for the Railway Institute's designers in building this car was the dimensions and weight of the sacred mirror.
The sacred mirror is an incarnation of the sun goddess ''[[Amaterasu]]'', and even the Crown Prince was not allowed to stand before it. Even if it was necessary by car design, the public was not allowed to see or touch it. When they asked for permission to measure, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] initially refused. Later, only dimensional measurements were permitted, but weight measurements, which required lifting (touching) the sacred mirror and placing it on a scale, were finally not permitted.
The engineers therefore decided to extrapolate from previous literature. In 1868, this mirror was carried by people by hand from Kyoto to Tokyo. According to the records, : Sixteen young men carried the [[:en:en:Litter (vehicle)|en:Litter (vehicle)]] with the sacred mirror eastward on the ''[[Tōkaidō (road)|Tōkaidō]]'' road, all of them sweating from the weight of the litter. The weight of the sacred mirror was estimated from this description. They then designed the installation stand and transport equipment based on a fairly high estimate.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
===Bringing in the sacred mirror=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha and Ohaguruma 04.png|thumb|Transfer from litter to car (sectional drawing).]]
At the time of Emperor Showa's accession to the throne, a manual for transferring the mirror was prepared and careful practice was conducted. * Tokyo Station: 4 times in May, September, October, and November 1928. * Nagoya Station: October 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 18, 22, 26, November 5, 24. (13th and 18th are for trial run) * Oy Manufacturing Plant: 2 times each by Tokyo, Nagoya, and Osaka station staff upon completion of train refurbishment in October. * Trial runs between Tokyo and Kyoto: October 13–19 and 22–28.<ref name="sr130"/>
===Other=== The braking device is a [[:en:Vacuum brake|Vacuum brake]]<ref name="rj180-149"/>
== Operation == After its manufacture, it was used for the coronation ceremony of Emperor Taisho in 1915, carrying the sacred mirror from Tokyo to Kyoto and back; it was also used for the coronation ceremony of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]] in 1928, with an updated interior and other improvements.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
The decision was made to discontinue use of this car in October 1959. Therefore, this car carried the sacred mirror only four times, including the round trip on the occasion of the coronation ceremonies of the Taisho and Showa Emperors.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
After it was taken out of service, it was stored in the [[:en:Takao Station (Tokyo)|Asakawa Garage]].<ref name="rj180-144"/>
== Afterward ==
In June 1963, since the Asakawa garage was to be closed, the car was moved to the Imperial garage in the Oy factory.<ref name="rj180-144"/>
In 1981, it was reported that the car smelled of cypress as one entered the interior.<ref name="rj180-148"/> Also, while cars for human built around the same time were converted to automatic air brakes, this car has been kept with vacuum brakes.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
In 2019, the law had changed, the [[Enthronement of the Japanese emperor]] took place in not Kyoto but Tokyo . The equipment for installing the sacred mirror during the accession ceremony, which had been permanently located in Kyoto, was transported to Tokyo. This eliminated the need to transport the sacred mirror by rail.
In 2023, the stored cars were moved before the demolition of the Imperial Garage began, but the new storage location has not been disclosed for security reasons.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/241525 |title=大正時代のれんが造り「御料車庫」の解体が始まる 失われる東京・品川の鉄道遺構 JR東日本が再開発 |trans-title= Dismantling of the Taisho Era brick Goryo Garage, a lost railroad structure in Shinagawa, Tokyo, to be redeveloped by East Japan Railway |publisher=東京新聞 |date=2023-04-02 |accessdate=2023-05-11}}</ref>
==References== {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="rj180-144">[[#Tanabe180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.144]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-148">[[#Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻 180号 p.148]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-149">[[# Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.149]]</ref> <ref name="sr130">[[# sRecord1|Ministry of Railways (1932) p.130]]</ref> }} * {{Cite journal|author=田邉幸夫 |year=1982 |volume=2|title=車両とともに30th year |trans-title= 30 years with the car|journal=鉄道ジャーナル |issue=180 |pages= 144–149 |publisher=鉄道ジャーナル社 |ref = Tanabe 180}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 附図 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor=[[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Ministry of Railways (Japan)]] |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor Appendix|year=1930|page=20}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 上巻 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor volume 1|year=1932|ref=sRecord1}}

At the back of the mirror room (on the opposite side of the entrance), there is a 385 mm wide passageway separated by a wall, allowing the attendant to go between the third and fifth rooms without passing through the mirror room.<ref name="rj180-148"/>
Rooms 1 and 2 have fixed long benches on both sides. Room 3 also has long benches, but with folding armrests, each can be separated into three seats. The surface materials of the attendant's room are [[:en:Oak|Oak]] and [[:en:Sawtooth oak|Sawtooth oak]]. The ceiling panels are [[:en:Maple|Maple]], and the window frames are [[teak]].<ref name="rj180-149"/>
The fourth room is the room for the sacred mirror. The mirror room has 2,438 mm wide folding doors. The mirrors are put in and out through these doors. After the doors are closed, parts of the chrysanthemum flower, the imperial emblem, are attached to the joints. The mirror room has a [[:en:Coffered ceiling|Coffered ceiling]]. The interior is ''[[Shinden-zukuri]]'', and most of the material is just ''[[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki]]'' without paint. The metal parts are [[:en:Gold plated|Gold plated]].<ref name="rj180-148"/>
Room 5 has a total of four seats, two on each side.
Room 6 has a small room with a toilet and handwash stand in one corner. This toilet bowl is painted in black [[:en:Lacquer|Lacquer]]. The outside of the handwash bowl is painted with black lacquer and the inside with vermilion lacquer. It is equipped with a white wooden ladle.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
Room 7 has almost the same structure as room 1 and 2.
=== Estimate the mirror weight=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha 02.jpg|thumb|250px| Room for the sacred mirror]] The biggest concern for the Railway Institute's designers in building this car was the dimensions and weight of the sacred mirror.
The sacred mirror is an incarnation of the sun goddess ''[[Amaterasu]]'', and even the Crown Prince was not allowed to stand before it. Even if it was necessary by car design, the public was not allowed to see or touch it. When they asked for permission to measure, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] initially refused. Later, only dimensional measurements were permitted, but weight measurements, which required lifting (touching) the sacred mirror and placing it on a scale, were finally not permitted.
The engineers therefore decided to extrapolate from previous literature. In 1868, this mirror was carried by people by hand from Kyoto to Tokyo. According to the records, : Sixteen young men carried the [[:en:en:Litter (vehicle)|en:Litter (vehicle)]] with the sacred mirror eastward on the ''[[Tōkaidō (road)|Tōkaidō]]'' road, all of them sweating from the weight of the litter. The weight of the sacred mirror was estimated from this description. They then designed the installation stand and transport equipment based on a fairly high estimate.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
===Bringing in the sacred mirror=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha and Ohaguruma 04.png|thumb|Transfer from litter to car (sectional drawing).]]
At the time of Emperor Showa's accession to the throne, a manual for transferring the mirror was prepared and careful practice was conducted. * Tokyo Station: 4 times in May, September, October, and November 1928. * Nagoya Station: October 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 18, 22, 26, November 5, 24. (13th and 18th are for trial run) * Oy Manufacturing Plant: 2 times each by Tokyo, Nagoya, and Osaka station staff upon completion of train refurbishment in October. * Trial runs between Tokyo and Kyoto: October 13–19 and 22–28.<ref name="sr130"/>
===Other=== The braking device is a [[:en:Vacuum brake|Vacuum brake]]<ref name="rj180-149"/>
== Operation == After its manufacture, it was used for the coronation ceremony of Emperor Taisho in 1915, carrying the sacred mirror from Tokyo to Kyoto and back; it was also used for the coronation ceremony of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]] in 1928, with an updated interior and other improvements.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
The decision was made to discontinue use of this car in October 1959. Therefore, this car carried the sacred mirror only four times, including the round trip on the occasion of the coronation ceremonies of the Taisho and Showa Emperors.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
After it was taken out of service, it was stored in the [[:en:Takao Station (Tokyo)|Asakawa Garage]].<ref name="rj180-144"/>
== Afterward ==
In June 1963, since the Asakawa garage was to be closed, the car was moved to the Imperial garage in the Oy factory.<ref name="rj180-144"/>
In 1981, it was reported that the car smelled of cypress as one entered the interior.<ref name="rj180-148"/> Also, while cars for human built around the same time were converted to automatic air brakes, this car has been kept with vacuum brakes.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
In 2019, the law had changed, the [[Enthronement of the Japanese emperor]] took place in not Kyoto but Tokyo . The equipment for installing the sacred mirror during the accession ceremony, which had been permanently located in Kyoto, was transported to Tokyo. This eliminated the need to transport the sacred mirror by rail.
In 2023, the stored cars were moved before the demolition of the Imperial Garage began, but the new storage location has not been disclosed for security reasons.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/241525 |title=大正時代のれんが造り「御料車庫」の解体が始まる 失われる東京・品川の鉄道遺構 JR東日本が再開発 |trans-title= Dismantling of the Taisho Era brick Goryo Garage, a lost railroad structure in Shinagawa, Tokyo, to be redeveloped by East Japan Railway |publisher=東京新聞 |date=2023-04-02 |accessdate=2023-05-11}}</ref>
==References== {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="rj180-144">[[#Tanabe180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.144]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-148">[[#Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻 180号 p.148]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-149">[[# Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.149]]</ref> <ref name="sr130">[[# sRecord1|Ministry of Railways (1932) p.130]]</ref> }} * {{Cite journal|author=田邉幸夫 |year=1982 |volume=2|title=車両とともに30th year |trans-title= 30 years with the car|journal=鉄道ジャーナル |issue=180 |pages= 144–149 |publisher=鉄道ジャーナル社 |ref = Tanabe 180}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 附図 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor=[[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Ministry of Railways (Japan)]] |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor Appendix|year=1930|page=20}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 上巻 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor volume 1|year=1932|ref=sRecord1}}
==See also== * [[:en:Royal train|Royal train]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox train |name =賢所乗御車 |image =Kashikodokoro Jogyosha 01.jpg |numberconstruction =self |trainlength =19,991 mm |width =2,590 mm |height =3,778 mm |carlength =19,748 mm |weight=31.49 t |carbody = wood |gauge =1,067 mm |bogies =three axles |brakes =[[:en:Vacuum brake|Vacuum brake]] |manufacturer =[[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Japanese Ministry of Railways]] Oy factory }} '''Kashikodokoro Riding Car''' (賢所乗御車, かしこどころじょうぎょしゃ) is a [[:en:Passenger railroad car|''Passenger'' railroad car]] manufactured by the [[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Japanese Ministry of Railways]] for the transportation of the sacred mirror, ''[[Yata no Kagami]]'' in 1915.<ref name="rj180-148"/>
== Background of Production == The sacred mirror, ''[[Yata no Kagami]]'' is one of the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures in Japan]]. During the [[Enthronement of the Japanese emperor]], the emperor must perform several ceremonies in front of this mirror.
When the Emperor before Emperor Taisho ascended to the throne, they lived in Kyoto, so there was no need to transport the sacred mirror far. When the Emperor moved from Kyoto to Tokyo, there was still no railroad connecting Kyoto and Tokyo, so the sacred mirror was carried by hand along with other sacred objects. Later, when the previous emperor died on July 30, 1912, the accession ceremony of Emperor Taisho, who succeeded to the throne, was to take place at the Kyoto Imperial Palace on November 10, 1915. This was because the [[:en:Imperial Household Law|Imperial Household Law]] of that time stipulated that the accession ceremony be held in Kyoto. This necessitated the temporary transfer of the sacred mirror needed for the ceremony to Kyoto. For this reason, a railroad car was built to carry the sacred mirror, along with car for the emperor and empress, a car for the empress, and a dining car.
There were three types of [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Imperial Regalia of Japan]]: mirror, sword, and jewel. Sword and jewel were loaded on the car for the emperor, but mirror was considered separate objects in which the god resided, so a special car was needed. <ref name="rj180-148"/>。
== Car overview == [[File:賢所乗御車図面.png|thumb|Top view drawing. Left is the front side.]]
===Interior=== In Japanese railroads, the entrance to the station platform is located on the left side of the train. The attendant and mirror also board this car from the left side.
The car is divided into seven rooms. A mirror is placed in the center (4th) room, and attendants ride in the three rooms in front of and behind the mirror room. At the back of the mirror room (on the opposite side of the entrance), there is a 385 mm wide passageway separated by a wall, allowing the attendant to go between the third and fifth rooms without passing through the mirror room.<ref name="rj180-148"/>
Rooms 1 and 2 have fixed long benches on both sides. Room 3 also has long benches, but with folding armrests, each can be separated into three seats. The surface materials of the attendant's room are [[:en:Oak|Oak]] and [[:en:Sawtooth oak|Sawtooth oak]]. The ceiling panels are [[:en:Maple|Maple]], and the window frames are [[:en:Teak|Teak]].<ref name="rj180-149"/>
The fourth room is the room for the sacred mirror. The mirror room has 2,438 mm wide folding doors. The mirrors are put in and out through these doors. After the doors are closed, parts of the chrysanthemum flower, the imperial emblem, are attached to the joints. The mirror room has a [[:en:Coffered ceiling|Coffered ceiling]]. The interior is ''[[Shinden-zukuri]]'', and most of the material is just ''[[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki]]'' without paint. The metal parts are [[:en:Gold plated|Gold plated]].<ref name="rj180-148"/>
Room 5 has a total of four seats, two on each side.
Room 6 has a small room with a toilet and handwash stand in one corner. This toilet bowl is painted in black [[:en:Lacquer|Lacquer]]. The outside of the handwash bowl is painted with black lacquer and the inside with vermilion lacquer. It is equipped with a white wooden ladle.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
Room 7 has almost the same structure as room 1 and 2.
=== Estimate the mirror weight=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha 02.jpg|thumb|250px| Room for the sacred mirror]] The biggest concern for the Railway Institute's designers in building this car was the dimensions and weight of the sacred mirror.
The sacred mirror is an incarnation of the sun goddess ''[[Amaterasu]]'', and even the Crown Prince was not allowed to stand before it. Even if it was necessary by car design, the public was not allowed to see or touch it. When they asked for permission to measure, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] initially refused. Later, only dimensional measurements were permitted, but weight measurements, which required lifting (touching) the sacred mirror and placing it on a scale, were finally not permitted.
The engineers therefore decided to extrapolate from previous literature. In 1868, this mirror was carried by people by hand from Kyoto to Tokyo. According to the records, : Sixteen young men carried the [[:en:en:Litter (vehicle)|en:Litter (vehicle)]] with the sacred mirror eastward on the ''[[Tōkaidō (road)|Tōkaidō]]'' road, all of them sweating from the weight of the litter. The weight of the sacred mirror was estimated from this description. They then designed the installation stand and transport equipment based on a fairly high estimate.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
===Bringing in the sacred mirror=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha and Ohaguruma 04.png|thumb|Transfer from litter to car (sectional drawing).]]
At the time of Emperor Showa's accession to the throne, a manual for transferring the mirror was prepared and careful practice was conducted. * Tokyo Station: 4 times in May, September, October, and November 1928. * Nagoya Station: October 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 18, 22, 26, November 5, 24. (13th and 18th are for trial run) * Oy Manufacturing Plant: 2 times each by Tokyo, Nagoya, and Osaka station staff upon completion of train refurbishment in October. * Trial runs between Tokyo and Kyoto: October 13–19 and 22–28.<ref name="sr130"/>
===Other=== The braking device is a [[:en:Vacuum brake|Vacuum brake]]<ref name="rj180-149"/>
== Operation == After its manufacture, it was used for the coronation ceremony of Emperor Taisho in 1915, carrying the sacred mirror from Tokyo to Kyoto and back; it was also used for the coronation ceremony of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]] in 1928, with an updated interior and other improvements.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
The decision was made to discontinue use of this car in October 1959. Therefore, this car carried the sacred mirror only four times, including the round trip on the occasion of the coronation ceremonies of the Taisho and Showa Emperors.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
After it was taken out of service, it was stored in the [[:en:Takao Station (Tokyo)|Asakawa Garage]].<ref name="rj180-144"/>
== Afterward ==
In June 1963, since the Asakawa garage was to be closed, the car was moved to the Imperial garage in the Oy factory.<ref name="rj180-144"/>
In 1981, it was reported that the car smelled of cypress as one entered the interior.<ref name="rj180-148"/> Also, while cars for human built around the same time were converted to automatic air brakes, this car has been kept with vacuum brakes.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
In 2019, the law had changed, the [[Enthronement of the Japanese emperor]] took place in not Kyoto but Tokyo . The equipment for installing the sacred mirror during the accession ceremony, which had been permanently located in Kyoto, was transported to Tokyo. This eliminated the need to transport the sacred mirror by rail.
In 2023, the stored cars were moved before the demolition of the Imperial Garage began, but the new storage location has not been disclosed for security reasons.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/241525 |title=大正時代のれんが造り「御料車庫」の解体が始まる 失われる東京・品川の鉄道遺構 JR東日本が再開発 |trans-title= Dismantling of the Taisho Era brick Goryo Garage, a lost railroad structure in Shinagawa, Tokyo, to be redeveloped by East Japan Railway |publisher=東京新聞 |date=2023-04-02 |accessdate=2023-05-11}}</ref>

When the Emperor moved from Kyoto to Tokyo, there was still no railroad connecting Kyoto and Tokyo, so the sacred mirror was carried by hand along with other sacred objects. Later, when the previous emperor died on July 30, 1912, the accession ceremony of Emperor Taisho, who succeeded to the throne, was to take place at the Kyoto Imperial Palace on November 10, 1915. This was because the [[:en:Imperial Household Law|Imperial Household Law]] of that time stipulated that the accession ceremony be held in Kyoto. This necessitated the temporary transfer of the sacred mirror needed for the ceremony to Kyoto. For this reason, a railroad car was built to carry the sacred mirror, along with car for the emperor and empress, a car for the empress, and a dining car.
There were three types of [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Imperial Regalia of Japan]]: mirror, sword, and jewel. Sword and jewel were loaded on the car for the emperor, but mirror was considered separate objects in which the god resided, so a special car was needed. <ref name="rj180-148"/>。
== Car overview == [[File:賢所乗御車図面.png|thumb|Top view drawing. Left is the front side.]]
===Interior=== In Japanese railroads, the entrance to the station platform is located on the left side of the train. The attendant and mirror also board this car from the left side.
The car is divided into seven rooms. A mirror is placed in the center (4th) room, and attendants ride in the three rooms in front of and behind the mirror room. At the back of the mirror room (on the opposite side of the entrance), there is a 385 mm wide passageway separated by a wall, allowing the attendant to go between the third and fifth rooms without passing through the mirror room.<ref name="rj180-148"/>
Rooms 1 and 2 have fixed long benches on both sides. Room 3 also has long benches, but with folding armrests, each can be separated into three seats. The surface materials of the attendant's room are [[:en:Oak|Oak]] and [[:en:Sawtooth oak|Sawtooth oak]]. The ceiling panels are [[:en:Maple|Maple]], and the window frames are [[:en:Teak|Teak]].<ref name="rj180-149"/>
The fourth room is the room for the sacred mirror. The mirror room has 2,438 mm wide folding doors. The mirrors are put in and out through these doors. After the doors are closed, parts of the chrysanthemum flower, the imperial emblem, are attached to the joints. The mirror room has a [[:en:Coffered ceiling|Coffered ceiling]]. The interior is ''[[:en:Shinden-zukuri|Shinden-zukuri]]'', and most of the material is just ''[[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki]]'' without paint. The metal parts are [[:en:Gold plated|Gold plated]].<ref name="rj180-148"/>
Room 5 has a total of four seats, two on each side.
Room 6 has a small room with a toilet and handwash stand in one corner. This toilet bowl is painted in black [[:en:Lacquer|Lacquer]]. The outside of the handwash bowl is painted with black lacquer and the inside with vermilion lacquer. It is equipped with a white wooden ladle.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
Room 7 has almost the same structure as room 1 and 2.
=== Estimate the mirror weight=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha 02.jpg|thumb|250px| Room for the sacred mirror]] The biggest concern for the Railway Institute's designers in building this car was the dimensions and weight of the sacred mirror.
The sacred mirror is an incarnation of the sun goddess ''[[Amaterasu]]'', and even the Crown Prince was not allowed to stand before it. Even if it was necessary by car design, the public was not allowed to see or touch it. When they asked for permission to measure, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] initially refused. Later, only dimensional measurements were permitted, but weight measurements, which required lifting (touching) the sacred mirror and placing it on a scale, were finally not permitted.
The engineers therefore decided to extrapolate from previous literature. In 1868, this mirror was carried by people by hand from Kyoto to Tokyo. According to the records, : Sixteen young men carried the [[:en:en:Litter (vehicle)|en:Litter (vehicle)]] with the sacred mirror eastward on the ''[[:en:Tōkaidō (road)|Tōkaidō]]'' road, all of them sweating from the weight of the litter. The weight of the sacred mirror was estimated from this description. They then designed the installation stand and transport equipment based on a fairly high estimate.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
===Bringing in the sacred mirror=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha and Ohaguruma 04.png|thumb|Transfer from litter to car (sectional drawing).]]
At the time of Emperor Showa's accession to the throne, a manual for transferring the mirror was prepared and careful practice was conducted. * Tokyo Station: 4 times in May, September, October, and November 1928. * Nagoya Station: October 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 18, 22, 26, November 5, 24. (13th and 18th are for trial run) * Oy Manufacturing Plant: 2 times each by Tokyo, Nagoya, and Osaka station staff upon completion of train refurbishment in October. * Trial runs between Tokyo and Kyoto: October 13–19 and 22–28.<ref name="sr130"/>
===Other=== The braking device is a [[:en:Vacuum brake|Vacuum brake]]<ref name="rj180-149"/>
== Operation == After its manufacture, it was used for the coronation ceremony of Emperor Taisho in 1915, carrying the sacred mirror from Tokyo to Kyoto and back; it was also used for the coronation ceremony of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]] in 1928, with an updated interior and other improvements.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
The decision was made to discontinue use of this car in October 1959. Therefore, this car carried the sacred mirror only four times, including the round trip on the occasion of the coronation ceremonies of the Taisho and Showa Emperors.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
After it was taken out of service, it was stored in the [[:en:Takao Station (Tokyo)|Asakawa Garage]].<ref name="rj180-144"/>
== Afterward ==
In June 1963, since the Asakawa garage was to be closed, the car was moved to the Imperial garage in the Oy factory.<ref name="rj180-144"/>
In 1981, it was reported that the car smelled of cypress as one entered the interior.<ref name="rj180-148"/> Also, while cars for human built around the same time were converted to automatic air brakes, this car has been kept with vacuum brakes.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
In 2019, the law had changed, the [[Enthronement of the Japanese emperor]] took place in not Kyoto but Tokyo . The equipment for installing the sacred mirror during the accession ceremony, which had been permanently located in Kyoto, was transported to Tokyo. This eliminated the need to transport the sacred mirror by rail.
In 2023, the stored cars were moved before the demolition of the Imperial Garage began, but the new storage location has not been disclosed for security reasons.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/241525 |title=大正時代のれんが造り「御料車庫」の解体が始まる 失われる東京・品川の鉄道遺構 JR東日本が再開発 |trans-title= Dismantling of the Taisho Era brick Goryo Garage, a lost railroad structure in Shinagawa, Tokyo, to be redeveloped by East Japan Railway |publisher=東京新聞 |date=2023-04-02 |accessdate=2023-05-11}}</ref>
==References== {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="rj180-144">[[#Tanabe180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.144]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-148">[[#Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻 180号 p.148]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-149">[[# Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.149]]</ref> <ref name="sr130">[[# sRecord1|Ministry of Railways (1932) p.130]]</ref> }} * {{Cite journal|author=田邉幸夫 |year=1982 |volume=2|title=車両とともに30th year |trans-title= 30 years with the car|journal=鉄道ジャーナル |issue=180 |pages= 144–149 |publisher=鉄道ジャーナル社 |ref = Tanabe 180}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 附図 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor=[[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Ministry of Railways (Japan)]] |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor Appendix|year=1930|page=20}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 上巻 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor volume 1|year=1932|ref=sRecord1}}
==See also== * [[:en:Royal train|Royal train]]
{{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }}

==References== {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="rj180-144">[[#Tanabe180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.144]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-148">[[#Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻 180号 p.148]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-149">[[# Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.149]]</ref> <ref name="sr130">[[# sRecord1|Ministry of Railways (1932) p.130]]</ref> }} * {{Cite journal|author=田邉幸夫 |year=1982 |volume=2|title=車両とともに30th year |trans-title= 30 years with the car|journal=鉄道ジャーナル |issue=180 |pages= 144–149 |publisher=鉄道ジャーナル社 |ref = Tanabe 180}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 附図 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor=[[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Ministry of Railways (Japan)]] |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor Appendix|year=1930|page=20}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 上巻 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor volume 1|year=1932|ref=sRecord1}}

revision.comment: Bot: add wikidata link templates
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11635084]] Wikidata name: Kashikodokoro Jōgyosha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11635084]] Wikidata name: Kashikodokoro Jōgyosha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox train |name =賢所乗御車 |image =Kashikodokoro Jogyosha 01.jpg |numberconstruction =self |trainlength =19,991 mm |width =2,590 mm |height =3,778 mm |carlength =19,748 mm |weight=31.49 t |carbody = wood |gauge =1,067 mm |bogies =three axles |brakes =[[:en:Vacuum brake|Vacuum brake]] |manufacturer =[[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Japanese Ministry of Railways]] Oy factory }} '''Kashikodokoro Riding Car''' (賢所乗御車, かしこどころじょうぎょしゃ) is a [[:en:Passenger railroad car|''Passenger'' railroad car]] manufactured by the [[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Japanese Ministry of Railways]] for the transportation of the sacred mirror, ''[[Yata no Kagami]]'' in 1915.<ref name="rj180-148"/>
== Background of Production == The sacred mirror, ''[[Yata no Kagami]]'' is one of the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures in Japan]]. During the [[Enthronement of the Japanese emperor]], the emperor must perform several ceremonies in front of this mirror.
When the Emperor before Emperor Taisho ascended to the throne, they lived in Kyoto, so there was no need to transport the sacred mirror far. When the Emperor moved from Kyoto to Tokyo, there was still no railroad connecting Kyoto and Tokyo, so the sacred mirror was carried by hand along with other sacred objects. Later, when the previous emperor died on July 30, 1912, the accession ceremony of Emperor Taisho, who succeeded to the throne, was to take place at the Kyoto Imperial Palace on November 10, 1915. This was because the [[:en:Imperial Household Law|Imperial Household Law]] of that time stipulated that the accession ceremony be held in Kyoto. This necessitated the temporary transfer of the sacred mirror needed for the ceremony to Kyoto. For this reason, a railroad car was built to carry the sacred mirror, along with car for the emperor and empress, a car for the empress, and a dining car.
There were three types of [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Imperial Regalia of Japan]]: mirror, sword, and jewel. Sword and jewel were loaded on the car for the emperor, but mirror was considered separate objects in which the god resided, so a special car was needed. <ref name="rj180-148"/>。
== Car overview == [[File:賢所乗御車図面.png|thumb|Top view drawing. Left is the front side.]]
===Interior=== In Japanese railroads, the entrance to the station platform is located on the left side of the train. The attendant and mirror also board this car from the left side.
The car is divided into seven rooms. A mirror is placed in the center (4th) room, and attendants ride in the three rooms in front of and behind the mirror room. At the back of the mirror room (on the opposite side of the entrance), there is a 385 mm wide passageway separated by a wall, allowing the attendant to go between the third and fifth rooms without passing through the mirror room.<ref name="rj180-148"/>
Rooms 1 and 2 have fixed long benches on both sides. Room 3 also has long benches, but with folding armrests, each can be separated into three seats. The surface materials of the attendant's room are [[:en:Oak|Oak]] and [[:en:Sawtooth oak|Sawtooth oak]]. The ceiling panels are [[:en:Maple|Maple]], and the window frames are [[:en:Teak|Teak]].<ref name="rj180-149"/>
The fourth room is the room for the sacred mirror. The mirror room has 2,438 mm wide folding doors. The mirrors are put in and out through these doors. After the doors are closed, parts of the chrysanthemum flower, the imperial emblem, are attached to the joints. The mirror room has a [[:en:Coffered ceiling|Coffered ceiling]]. The interior is ''[[:en:Shinden-zukuri|Shinden-zukuri]]'', and most of the material is just ''[[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki]]'' without paint. The metal parts are [[:en:Gold plated|Gold plated]].<ref name="rj180-148"/>
Room 5 has a total of four seats, two on each side.
Room 6 has a small room with a toilet and handwash stand in one corner. This toilet bowl is painted in black [[:en:Lacquer|Lacquer]]. The outside of the handwash bowl is painted with black lacquer and the inside with vermilion lacquer. It is equipped with a white wooden ladle.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
Room 7 has almost the same structure as room 1 and 2.
=== Estimate the mirror weight=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha 02.jpg|thumb|250px| Room for the sacred mirror]] The biggest concern for the Railway Institute's designers in building this car was the dimensions and weight of the sacred mirror.
The sacred mirror is an incarnation of the sun goddess ''[[Amaterasu]]'', and even the Crown Prince was not allowed to stand before it. Even if it was necessary by car design, the public was not allowed to see or touch it. When they asked for permission to measure, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] initially refused. Later, only dimensional measurements were permitted, but weight measurements, which required lifting (touching) the sacred mirror and placing it on a scale, were finally not permitted.
The engineers therefore decided to extrapolate from previous literature. In 1868, this mirror was carried by people by hand from Kyoto to Tokyo. According to the records, : Sixteen young men carried the [[:en:en:Litter (vehicle)|en:Litter (vehicle)]] with the sacred mirror eastward on the ''[[:en:Tōkaidō (road)|Tōkaidō]]'' road, all of them sweating from the weight of the litter. The weight of the sacred mirror was estimated from this description. They then designed the installation stand and transport equipment based on a fairly high estimate.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
===Bringing in the sacred mirror=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha and Ohaguruma 04.png|thumb|Transfer from litter to car (sectional drawing).]]
At the time of Emperor Showa's accession to the throne, a manual for transferring the mirror was prepared and careful practice was conducted. * Tokyo Station: 4 times in May, September, October, and November 1928. * Nagoya Station: October 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 18, 22, 26, November 5, 24. (13th and 18th are for trial run) * Oy Manufacturing Plant: 2 times each by Tokyo, Nagoya, and Osaka station staff upon completion of train refurbishment in October. * Trial runs between Tokyo and Kyoto: October 13–19 and 22–28.<ref name="sr130"/>
===Other=== The braking device is a [[:en:Vacuum brake|Vacuum brake]]<ref name="rj180-149"/>
== Operation == After its manufacture, it was used for the coronation ceremony of Emperor Taisho in 1915, carrying the sacred mirror from Tokyo to Kyoto and back; it was also used for the coronation ceremony of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]] in 1928, with an updated interior and other improvements.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
The decision was made to discontinue use of this car in October 1959. Therefore, this car carried the sacred mirror only four times, including the round trip on the occasion of the coronation ceremonies of the Taisho and Showa Emperors.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
After it was taken out of service, it was stored in the [[:en:Takao Station (Tokyo)|Asakawa Garage]].<ref name="rj180-144"/>
== Afterward ==
In June 1963, since the Asakawa garage was to be closed, the car was moved to the Imperial garage in the Oy factory.<ref name="rj180-144"/>
In 1981, it was reported that the car smelled of cypress as one entered the interior.<ref name="rj180-148"/> Also, while cars for human built around the same time were converted to automatic air brakes, this car has been kept with vacuum brakes.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
In 2019, the law had changed, the [[Enthronement of the Japanese emperor]] took place in not Kyoto but Tokyo . The equipment for installing the sacred mirror during the accession ceremony, which had been permanently located in Kyoto, was transported to Tokyo. This eliminated the need to transport the sacred mirror by rail.
In 2023, the stored cars were moved before the demolition of the Imperial Garage began, but the new storage location has not been disclosed for security reasons.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/241525 |title=大正時代のれんが造り「御料車庫」の解体が始まる 失われる東京・品川の鉄道遺構 JR東日本が再開発 |trans-title= Dismantling of the Taisho Era brick Goryo Garage, a lost railroad structure in Shinagawa, Tokyo, to be redeveloped by East Japan Railway |publisher=東京新聞 |date=2023-04-02 |accessdate=2023-05-11}}</ref>
==References== {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="rj180-144">[[#Tanabe180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.144]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-148">[[#Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻 180号 p.148]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-149">[[# Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.149]]</ref> <ref name="sr130">[[# sRecord1|Ministry of Railways (1932) p.130]]</ref> }} * {{Cite journal|author=田邉幸夫 |year=1982 |volume=2|title=車両とともに30th year |trans-title= 30 years with the car|journal=鉄道ジャーナル |issue=180 |pages= 144–149 |publisher=鉄道ジャーナル社 |ref = Tanabe 180}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 附図 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor=[[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Ministry of Railways (Japan)]] |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor Appendix|year=1930|page=20}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 上巻 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor volume 1|year=1932|ref=sRecord1}}
==See also== * [[:en:Royal train|Royal train]]
{{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }}

=== Estimate the mirror weight=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha 02.jpg|thumb|250px| Room for the sacred mirror]] The biggest concern for the Railway Institute's designers in building this car was the dimensions and weight of the sacred mirror.
The sacred mirror is an incarnation of the sun goddess ''[[Amaterasu]]'', and even the Crown Prince was not allowed to stand before it. Even if it was necessary by car design, the public was not allowed to see or touch it. When they asked for permission to measure, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] initially refused. Later, only dimensional measurements were permitted, but weight measurements, which required lifting (touching) the sacred mirror and placing it on a scale, were finally not permitted.
The engineers therefore decided to extrapolate from previous literature. In 1868, this mirror was carried by people by hand from Kyoto to Tokyo. According to the records, : Sixteen young men carried the [[:en:en:Litter (vehicle)|en:Litter (vehicle)]] with the sacred mirror eastward on the ''[[:en:Tōkaidō (road)|Tōkaidō]]'' road, all of them sweating from the weight of the litter. The weight of the sacred mirror was estimated from this description. They then designed the installation stand and transport equipment based on a fairly high estimate.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
===Bringing in the sacred mirror=== [[File:Kashikodokoro Jogyosha and Ohaguruma 04.png|thumb|Transfer from litter to car (sectional drawing).]]
At the time of Emperor Showa's accession to the throne, a manual for transferring the mirror was prepared and careful practice was conducted. * Tokyo Station: 4 times in May, September, October, and November 1928. * Nagoya Station: October 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 18, 22, 26, November 5, 24. (13th and 18th are for trial run) * Oy Manufacturing Plant: 2 times each by Tokyo, Nagoya, and Osaka station staff upon completion of train refurbishment in October. * Trial runs between Tokyo and Kyoto: October 13–19 and 22–28.<ref name="sr130"/>
===Other=== The braking device is a [[:en:Vacuum brake|Vacuum brake]]<ref name="rj180-149"/>
== Operation == After its manufacture, it was used for the coronation ceremony of Emperor Taisho in 1915, carrying the sacred mirror from Tokyo to Kyoto and back; it was also used for the coronation ceremony of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]] in 1928, with an updated interior and other improvements.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
The decision was made to discontinue use of this car in October 1959. Therefore, this car carried the sacred mirror only four times, including the round trip on the occasion of the coronation ceremonies of the Taisho and Showa Emperors.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
After it was taken out of service, it was stored in the [[:en:Takao Station (Tokyo)|Asakawa Garage]].<ref name="rj180-144"/>
== Afterward ==
In June 1963, since the Asakawa garage was to be closed, the car was moved to the Imperial garage in the Oy factory.<ref name="rj180-144"/>
In 1981, it was reported that the car smelled of cypress as one entered the interior.<ref name="rj180-148"/> Also, while cars for human built around the same time were converted to automatic air brakes, this car has been kept with vacuum brakes.<ref name="rj180-149"/>
In 2019, the law had changed, the [[Enthronement of the Japanese emperor]] took place in not Kyoto but Tokyo . The equipment for installing the sacred mirror during the accession ceremony, which had been permanently located in Kyoto, was transported to Tokyo. This eliminated the need to transport the sacred mirror by rail.
In 2023, the stored cars were moved before the demolition of the Imperial Garage began, but the new storage location has not been disclosed for security reasons.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/241525 |title=大正時代のれんが造り「御料車庫」の解体が始まる 失われる東京・品川の鉄道遺構 JR東日本が再開発 |trans-title= Dismantling of the Taisho Era brick Goryo Garage, a lost railroad structure in Shinagawa, Tokyo, to be redeveloped by East Japan Railway |publisher=東京新聞 |date=2023-04-02 |accessdate=2023-05-11}}</ref>
==References== {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="rj180-144">[[#Tanabe180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.144]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-148">[[#Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻 180号 p.148]]</ref> <ref name="rj180-149">[[# Tanabe 180|『鉄道ジャーナル』通巻180号 p.149]]</ref> <ref name="sr130">[[# sRecord1|Ministry of Railways (1932) p.130]]</ref> }} * {{Cite journal|author=田邉幸夫 |year=1982 |volume=2|title=車両とともに30th year |trans-title= 30 years with the car|journal=鉄道ジャーナル |issue=180 |pages= 144–149 |publisher=鉄道ジャーナル社 |ref = Tanabe 180}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 附図 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor=[[:en:Ministry of Railways (Japan)|Ministry of Railways (Japan)]] |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor Appendix|year=1930|page=20}} * {{Cite book |title=昭和大礼記録 上巻 |publisher= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |editor= Ministry of Railways (Japan) |trans-title= Record of the enthronement of Showa emperor volume 1|year=1932|ref=sRecord1}}
==See also== * [[:en:Royal train|Royal train]]
